name: North Shore Trust And Savings
description_short: North Shore Trust & Savings is a community bank serving the Waukegan and northern Illinois area since 1921, offering checking, savings, CDs, mortgages, and financial literacy resources.
description_long:
North Shore Trust & Savings has operated as a local community bank in the Waukegan area for over 100 years, established in 1921. The bank has persisted through decades of consolidation and competition, maintaining its presence as an independent institution serving Lake County. The company emphasizes its longevity and deep community roots in its marketing materials.

The bank offers a full suite of retail banking services including three checking account tiers (Free Checking, Fresh Start Checking, Premium Checking), multiple savings products (Passbook Savings, Statement Savings, Money Market Accounts, CDs, IRAs, and a Kids Club Savings Account), and mortgage services through a partnership with Oak Leaf Community Mortgage. Current promotional offerings include 13-month CDs at 3.80% APY and 30-month CDs at 2.78% APY with $1,000 minimum deposits. Digital services include online banking, mobile banking, eStatements, online bill pay, and mobile wallet support.

North Shore distinguishes itself through local decision-making, community focus, and educational resources. The bank provides financial literacy education covering topics like budgeting, home buying, credit, retirement planning, and life changes. They operate three physical branch locations in the Waukegan and Lindenhurst areas with extended hours, and offer an ATM locator service. The bank also emphasizes customer service through team spotlights and maintains investor relations programs.

As a traditional community bank, North Shore offers stability and personalized service but operates with limited geographic reach compared to national competitors. The company's mortgage services are outsourced to Oak Leaf Community Mortgage, suggesting limited in-house lending capacity. While rates and fees are not fully detailed on their website, the emphasis on "less money on fees" suggests competitive positioning. The bank appears well-suited for customers prioritizing local banking relationships and community banking principles over potentially lower rates or digital-only convenience.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with promotional rates
Mortgage services (through Oak Leaf Community Mortgage partnership)
pros: Over 100 years of continuous operation since 1921, demonstrating institutional stability and local presence
Three physical branch locations with extended hours (drive-up until 5 p.m. weekdays, 1 p.m. Saturdays)
Competitive CD rates: 3.80% APY on 13-month and 2.78% APY on 30-month CDs with low $1,000 minimums
Multiple checking account options (Free, Fresh Start, Premium) designed for different customer needs
Comprehensive financial literacy resources covering budgeting, home buying, credit, retirement, and life changes
Full-service banking including checking, savings, CDs, IRAs, Money Market Accounts, and Kids Club Savings
Modern digital banking: online banking, mobile banking, eStatements, online bill pay, and mobile wallets
cons: Limited geographic footprint—only three branches in Waukegan and Lindenhurst area; no statewide or national presence
Mortgage services outsourced to third-party Oak Leaf Community Mortgage rather than offered directly in-house
Website does not display specific fee schedules, interest rates for checking accounts, or detailed terms and conditions
No mention of business banking services despite 'Banking For Business' navigation link—details not provided
Weekend lobby hours very limited (Saturday by appointment only 9 a.m.–12 p.m.)
best_for: Northern Illinois residents and business owners seeking community banking with local decision-making
Savers interested in competitive CD rates with low minimum deposits and relationship-based service
Families wanting financial literacy support and a Kids Club Savings Account for teaching money management
Home buyers in the Lake County area who value personalized mortgage guidance from established lenders
diagnosis: North Shore Trust & Savings is best for consumers in the northern Illinois area who prioritize local banking relationships, stability, and financial education over maximum convenience or the lowest rates. The main caveat is geographic limitation—customers outside the Waukegan and Lindenhurst areas will find this bank impractical, and mortgage borrowers should expect service through a third-party lender.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
